data.title: UK pest control firm Rentokil names US-based CEO
data.description:
British pest control firm Rentokil Initial named logistics firm OnTrac's Mike Duffy as its new chief executive officer on Tuesday, succeeding Andy Ransom in March upon his retirement.

Duffy, a U.S. citizen, will be based in North America, Rentokil's biggest market. Revenue in the U.S. has been picking up for the FTSE-100 company after a challenging period that Ransom steered the company through.

Rentokil's shares, which have quadrupled in value since Ransom became CEO in October 2013, were marginally up in early trading at 475.2 pence.

Duffy has held various leadership roles with large U.S. businesses in his 25 years-plus career in consumer-facing and business-to-business industries, including at FleetPride, a heavy-duty trucks parts distributor, and C&S Wholesale Grocers.

Outgoing CEO Ransom, who has been with the firm for about 18 years, also led Rentokil's $6.7 billion acquisition of Terminix in 2022, combining the two companies into a global leader in the pest control industry.

data.title: European Stocks Break New Highs
data.description:
The STOXX 50 edged up 0.1% and the STOXX 600 rose 0.3% on Tuesday, extending Monday’s gains and reaching a fresh record high, mirroring the positive momentum seen the previous day.

Investor sentiment was supported by attractive valuations and fresh corporate updates, while traders continued to monitor geopolitical risks involving Greenland and Iran, as well as concerns over the Federal Reserve’s independence.

Shares of Orsted surged 5.6% after a US judge cleared the company to resume work on its nearly completed Revolution Wind project.

ASML Holding (+1.6%), Novo Nordisk (+0.9%), Inditex (+0.7%), Airbus (+0.7%), and Banco Santander (+1.1%) also posted gains, while the defense and aerospace sector extended its rally.

On the downside, Vinci (-3.0%) and AstraZeneca (-0.8%) were among the worst performers.

data.title: Czech Republic Inflation Rate Confirmed at 2.1%
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in the Czech Republic held steady at 2.1% in December 2025, unchanged from November, confirming preliminary estimates.

Prices continued to increase for alcoholic beverages and tobacco (4.1% vs 4% in November), housing and utilities (1.9% vs 1.7%), furnishings, household equipment and maintenance (1.4% vs 1.0%), health (3.3% vs 2.9%), restaurants and hotels (4.7% vs 4.6%), and miscellaneous goods and services (3.5% vs 3.2%).

Meanwhile, inflation eased for food and non-alcoholic beverages (1.7% vs 2.2%), communication (0.4% vs 0.5%), and recreation and culture (3.6% vs 3.7%), while cost in education remained steady at 4.1%.

Transport costs also declined (-0.2% vs 0.4%), while deflation persisted for clothing and footwear at 2.1%.

On a monthly basis, consumer prices fell by 0.3% in December, unchanged from the previous month.

data.title: UK's THG jumps after reporting first revenue growth since 2021
data.description:
** Shares of British e-commerce firm THG up 7.6% at 51.6p

** Stock among top gainers on FTSE mid-caps , which is up 0.10%

** THG reported FY revenue growth of 2.3%, its first rise since 2021, rebounding from a 2.5% H1 decline

** Co also said its FY 2025 core profit expectations remain unchanged and is in line with the company's consensus range

** Q4 trading was the strongest quarter this year, with good momentum into FY26 and clear drivers for the year ahead. We leave our profit forecasts unchanged - Peel Hunt

** YTD, stock up 11.6% and FTSE mid-caps up 2.5%

data.title: SCG Packaging's Core Earnings Could Recover in 1Q — Market Talk
data.description: SCG Packaging's core earnings are likely to recover in 1Q, say DBS Group Research's Duladeth Bik in a note. He expects the Thai packaging manufacturer to see resilient demand for packaging material in Vietnam and Indonesia. Printing and writing paper demand is also likely rise in the near term thanks to the upcoming Thai election period, he says. However, SCG's quarterly sales volumes may be weighed by planned maintenance for a dissolving-pulp plant, he adds. DBS maintains its hold rating on the company, noting slower-than-expected recovery in demand from Europe and China and pressure on packaging-product prices from Chinese and Indian competitors. DBS retains its target price at THB17.00. Shares rise 1.1% to THB18.80. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Lapin Kumi, part of Citira, has signed an agreement to acquire Päijät-Kumi
data.description:
Lapin Kumi, Citira’s Finnish tire services chain, has signed an agreement to acquire Päijät-Kumi, an established provider of tire services in Jyväskylä, central Finland. The acqusition marks the next step in Citira’s strategy to build Lapin Kumi to service all major transport routes across Finland.

“Päijät-Kumi is a well-managed company with a strong history and an established position in the industry. Their longstanding efforts and professional staff provide an excellent platform for the future. We want to ensure that their achievements will continue to be built upon in the years to come,” says Ville Ruokanen, CEO of Lapin Kumi.

Päijät-Kumi is a family-owned business founded in 1991. In 2008, Päijät-Kumi entered a new phase when ownership was passed to the next generation, and in 2022, Päijät-Kumi moved to a new location in Keljon Eteläportti.

“We feel that 2026 is the right time to become part of the expanding Lapin Kumi group. Through this transaction, we will gain even better opportunities to develop our operations and to offer our customers the best possible service. We are very much looking forward to the journey ahead together with our entire staff,” says Jussi Vuoristo of Päijät-Kumi.

Päijät-Kumi will continue to operate in its current location with the same team, as part of Lapin Kumi. The current owners will become co-owners of Citira. The transaction will be completed in January 2026.

Contact

Ville Roukanen, CEO Lapin Kumi & Head of Finland Citira

Email: press@citira.com

About Lapin Kumi

Lapin Kumi is an independent tire service chain in Finland, founded in Roveniemi and run by the Ruokanen family since 1947. The current CEO, Ville Ruokanen, represents the third generation in the family business. Ville’s grandfather, Veikko Keskiruokanen, started the company by repairing rubber boots. Lapin Kumi is part of Citira.

About Citira

Citira is a circular tire management provider. Our company offers tire service, collection and retreading of worn-out tires, as well as sale and delivery of replacement tires. Our inhouse logistics enables a circular flow of products – delivering replacement tires, while collecting used casings.

Citira strives to make tire management convenient and sustainable for transport companies and vehicle users. Our company supports customers in gaining more uptime, reduce environmental impact, lower costs, and meet safety requirements.

Citira consists of 120+ directly operated service stations, 39 affiliated service stations, 5 retreading sites, 1000+ employees and inhouse logistics covering Northern Europe. Our mission is to keep society moving in a convenient, sustainable way.

For more info visit citira.com and follow us on Linkedin.

Attachments

Lapin Kumi, part of Citira, has signed an agreement to acquire Päijät-Kumi

data.title: Gilt Yields Rise, U.S. Inflation Data in Focus — Market Talk
data.description: Yields on U.K. government bonds rise, reversing Monday's decline when 10-year yields fell to 4.372%, their lowest level since April, Tradeweb data show. Focus is on U.S. inflation data set to be released at 1330 GMT. The data could signal the possible path of future interest-rate cuts by the U.S. Federal Reserve, with a stronger-than-expected reading lowering the chances of a near-term rate cut. Ten-year gilt yields rise around 2 basis points to last trade at 4.396%. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Europe open: Stoxx hits fresh high despite Trump tariff threat on Iran partners
data.description:
The pan-regional Stoxx 600 hit 611.78, a new intra-day high, and was up 0.09% at 611.52 at 0812 GMT. All major bourses made slight gains in early deals.

Trump said any country that traded with Tehran would face the tariff rate on exports to the US, as Washington mulled its response to the Islamic regime’s crackdown on anti-government protests that has left hundreds dead, according to human rights groups.

“Effective immediately, any Country doing business with the Islamic Republic of Iran will pay a Tariff of 25% on any and all business being done with the United States of America,” Trump said in his usual overnight round of social media pronouncements.

“This order is final and conclusive,” he added. Iran’s major trading partners include China – a large buyer of its oil - the United Arab Emirates and India.

Oil prices jumped, with Brent crude up 0.47% to $64.17 a barrel as investors fretted about the potential threat to supply from the world’s fifth largest producer.

In equity news, shares in Orsted rose nearly 6% after a US judge cleared the Danish renewables company to resume work on its $5bn Revolution Wind project near the Rhode Island coast in a legal setback for Trump who had moved to block it.

Whitbread gained as the Premier Inn owner said it expected a lower-than-anticipated hit from business rate hikes announced in the UK's autumn budget.

German fragrances maker Symrise surged as it launched a €400m share buyback and on Monday said it was in advanced talks to sell its terpenes business and that it would take a non-cash impairment of about €145m related to the sale.

Reporting by Frank Prenesti for Sharecast.com

data.title: The FTSE 100 Index Opens 0.03% Higher
data.description:
The FTSE 100 Index is picking up 3 points or 0.03 percent on Tuesday.

Gains are led by Whitbread (4.02%), Mondi (2.67%) and Diageo (2.18%).

Biggest losers are Admiral (-2.33%), Entain (-1.63%) and United Utilities (-1.40%).

data.title: Mozambique's Maputo port volumes hit record 32 million metric tons
data.description:
Mozambique's Maputo port, the country's largest, handled a record 32 million metric tons of cargo in 2025, driven by expanded capacity and higher freight rail volumes, the harbour’s operator said on Tuesday.

The port, which handles bulk shipments of minerals such as chrome, iron ore and coal as well as agricultural commodities, saw volumes rise from 30.9 million metric tons in 2024, the Maputo Port Development Company (MPDC) said in a statement. That figure was the previous record.

Cargo through Maputo has climbed 52% from 21 million metric tons in 2019, official data show, underscoring its growing role as a regional logistics hub.

The increase reflects capacity upgrades and improved rail links, as well as logistical bottlenecks in neighbouring South Africa that diverted some cargo to Mozambique. Rising mineral output in Zimbabwe also contributed to the gains.

Rail volumes into Maputo rose to 11.7 million metric tons in 2025 from 9.7 million the previous year, MPDC said.

Maputo port is operated under a concession by a partnership between state-owned Mozambican Railway Company and Portus Indico, a consortium formed by South Africa's Grindrod , Dubai-based DP World and local firm Gestores.

The concession, granted in 2003, was extended last year to 2058 and includes plans to expand capacity further.

data.title: South African rand eases on profit taking after gold-led gains
data.description:
The South African rand softened in early trade on Tuesday as investors took profits after the currency benefited in the previous session from a surge in gold prices.

At 0815 GMT the rand traded at 16.4250 against the dollar , about 0.2% weaker than its previous close.

It gained around 0.5% on Monday, boosted by gold which climbed more than 2% to an all-time high as investors sought the safe haven asset after the Trump administration opened a criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

Bullion also edged lower on Tuesday as some traders booked short-term profit.

Like other risk-sensitive currencies, the rand is expected to take cues from global drivers like the unrest in Iran in addition to concerns over the Fed's independence.

On the domestic front, traders are also tracking developments around a potential extension of the U.S. trade preference programme, the African Growth and Opportunity Act (AGOA).

The U.S. House of Representatives approved a three-year extension, but uncertainty remains over whether South Africa will be excluded, given strained diplomatic relations between Washington and Pretoria.

The bill will next go to the U.S. Senate.

On the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the Top-40 index was last up 0.2%.

South Africa's benchmark 2035 government bond (ZAR2035=) was flat in early deals, with the yield at 8.305%.

data.title: Diamyd Medical: a Swedish example of Nobel Prize science in action
data.description:
The 2025 Nobel Prize spotlighted peripheral immune tolerance, a principle increasingly shaping new strategies for autoimmune disease. In a conversation with BioStock, Diamyd Medical board member Dr. Karin Rosén shares why this field is now moving from discovery to development – and how the company is applying the science in autoimmune diabetes.

Read the article at biostock.se:

https://biostock.se/en/2026/01/diamyd-medical-ett-svenskt-exempel-pa-nobelprisbelonad-forskning-i-praktiken/

This is a press release fromBioStock - Connecting Innovation & Capital.https://www.biostock.se/

Follow BioStock

Stay up to date with the latest news, upcoming events, recent videos, and podcasts! Subscribe to our newsletters athttps://www.biostock.se/newsletter

Follow BioStock on social media:LinkedInandFacebook!

About BioStock

BioStock is the leading digital hub for the Nordic life science sector. Focusing on biotechnology, medical technology, diagnostics, and drug development, we provide daily news updates, in-depth analyses, exclusive interviews, and innovation-promoting networking events – all to keep you informed about industry developments.

BioStock – Connecting Innovation & Capital.

data.title: China Expected to Roll out More Stimulus in 2026 — Market Talk
data.description: The latest policy signals from China's December Politburo meetings point to continued commitment to supporting growth, with further stimulus likely ahead, HSBC analysts say in a note. They expect investment activity to pick up and for trade to stay robust. HSBC anticipates more clarity on medium-term growth strategies in 2026, with a key focus on lifting consumption's share of GDP to a potential target of 45% from the current 40%. Authorities are also expected to continue facilitating a gradual yuan appreciation. In the near term, policy support is likely to include consumption subsidies and credit measures such as interest subsidies on consumer loans, while broader social-welfare enhancements could help raise disposable incomes in the future. As investor confidence improves, HSBC projects a continued shift from cash into risk assets across China. (jason.chau@wsj.com)

data.title: Most Gulf bourses gain on oil
data.description:
Most major stock markets in the Gulf rose in early trade on Tuesday, helped by climbing oil prices, while investors assessed the possible impact on the dollar and interest rates of the U.S. administration's threats of a criminal indictment against Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

Oil prices - a catalyst for the Gulf's financial markets - extended gains as heightened concerns over Iran and potential supply disruptions outweighed the prospect of increased crude supply from Venezuela.

Dubai's main share index gained 0.6%, with top lender Emirates NBD rising 1%.

In Abu Dhabi, the index was up 0.3%.

In the U.S., President Donald Trump's administration appears to be on the offensive again against the Federal Reserve, reviving concerns about the central bank's independence and contributing to greater uncertainty over the economic outlook.

Gulf markets often track shifts in U.S. rate expectations, as most regional currencies are pegged to the dollar.

Further fuelling geopolitical worries, Trump said Monday that any country doing business with Iran will be hit with a 25% U.S. tariff, as Washington considers how to respond to Iran's crackdown on major anti-government protests.

Saudi Arabia's benchmark index was flat in choppy trade. Oil giant Saudi Aramco was up 0.7%.

The Qatari index gained 0.2%, with Qatar Islamic Bank up 0.7%.

data.title: SOPHARMA AD - Notification for sale of treasury shares
data.description:
POLISH FINANCIAL SUPERVISION AUTHORITY

UNI - EN REPORT No 14 / 2026

Date of issue: 2026-01-13

Short name of the issuer

SOPHARMA AD

Subject

Notification for sale of treasury shares

Official market - legal basis

Art. 56. 1. 2 of Act on Public Offering

Unofficial market - legal basis

Contents of the report:

Sofia, Bulgaria – According to the requirements of art. 17 of the Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 of

the European Parliament and of the Council, “Sopharma” AD (SFA: Bulgarian Stock Exchange, SPH:

Warsaw Stock Exchange) notifies that on January 12, 2026 the Company sold 40 600 (forty thousand and

six hundred) treasury shares representing 0.01% of the share capital of the Company, at a total

value of EUR 77 043.00 on the Bulgarian Stock Exchange, the average price per share was EUR 1.90.

data.title: Raspberry Pi Shares Fall After Memory Shortage Clouds Outlook
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Raspberry Pi Holdings shares fell after the company said a challenging memory supply environment makes its outlook uncertain beyond the first half.

Shares in the U.K. computer maker were down 7.9% at 2.67 pounds. Over the past twelve months, have fallen over 50%.

The company said Tuesday that its performance in the second half will depend on pricing trends for DRAM--device's main system memory--chips and how customers react to any further price increases, as well as on supply availability and the effectiveness of mitigating actions.

The build-out of infrastructure for artificial intelligence is rapidly absorbing supplies of memory chips and hard drives, triggering shortages that threaten to hinder manufacturers of consumer electronics.

For the first half, Raspberry Pi said it expects unit shipments to grow compared with the same period last year and profitability to be in line with its board's forecasts, which it didn't provide.

The company said it estimates 2025 adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization to be at least $45 million, up more than 20% compared with the prior year and ahead of a company-compiled consensus of $40.9 million.

Unit shipments totaled 7.6 million for 2025, with 4.0 million in the second half, it added. For 2024, the company reported unit volumes of 7 million.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: European Indexes Mixed on Gains for Banks, Losses in Construction — Market Talk
data.description: European indexes open mixed as investors await key inflation data from the U.S. The tech-dominated Dutch AEX is fastest out the gates, rising 0.5% as semiconductor companies gain. London's FTSE 100 is flat after notching another record close Monday, as housebuilders rally after Persimmon earnings. The German DAX also trades flat after a record close, as gains for chemicals company Symrise--up 5.4%--and retailer Zalando--up 5.9%--are cancelled out by further losses for autos and a 1.5% drop for Heidelberg Materials. Southern European banks climb, pushing the Spanish IBEX 35 up 0.4% while the Italian FTSE MIB trades flat, dragged by a 3.8% drop for shipbuilder Fincantieri. In Paris, construction companies push the CAC 40 down 0.2% as Eiffage and Vinci both drop around 3.3%. (josephmichael.stonor@wsj.com)

data.title: Interim sales, profits jump at Games Workshop
data.description:
Revenues at the blue chip came in at £332.1m in the 26 weeks to 30 November, up from £299.5m a year previously. Pre-tax profits rose to £140.8m from £126.8m.

Kevin Rountree, chief executive, called it a “record half-year performance”.

He continued: “We have again remained focused on delivering our strategic goal – to make the best fantasy miniatures in the world, to engage and inspire our customers and to sell our products globally at profit. We intend to do this forever.”

The company said it had seen profitable sales growth across its 23 core markets and in all three channels, driven by weekly releases of new miniatures.

It also opened more stores during the period, although Rountree acknowledge the rate of expansion had been slower than expected. Games Workshop now has 575 shops worldwide, including 134 in the UK and 202 in North America.

Tariffs impacted profits by around £6m during the period, in line with forecasts for a hit of around £12m over the full year.

Looking to the second half, Games Workshop said core sales in the December had been “slightly ahead” of the same month a year previously. It did not, however, provide any further guidance regarding the full year.

Founded in 1975 as a maker of traditional wooden board games such as backgammon, Games Workshop’s growth soared as it moved into miniature wargames. Played with figurines painted by dedicated hobbyists, the most well-know of its games is the hugely popular Warhammer.

In 2023 the Nottingham-based company signed a licensing deal with Amazon, which includes developing Warhammer films and television programmes.

The firm was promoted to the FTSE 100 last month.

data.title: ICICIPRULI: Strong profit growth, robust solvency, and leadership in digital and customer experience
data.description:
APE and VNB grew modestly year-over-year, with strong profit and AUM growth. Persistency and solvency ratios remain robust, while retail protection and annuity segments outperformed. Continued focus on digital, product innovation, and distribution expansion.

data.title: Shoe Zone Shares Plunge After Profit Hit by Weak Demand
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Shares in Shoe Zone plunged on Tuesday after the U.K. footwear retailer said profit in fiscal 2025 was hit by lower demand and it flagged challenges ahead.

In European morning trading, shares trade 22% lower at 52.50 pence. Over the past year, shares are down 27%, the lowest price in five years.

The retailer said it made pretax profit of 3.3 million pounds ($4.4 million) for the 52-week period ended Sept. 27 compared with 10.1 million pounds the previous year.

The decline reflected a drop in consumer confidence with discretionary spending remaining subdued, Shoe Zone said. "Persistent inflation, higher interest rates, and reduced disposable income contributed to negative economic and consumer sentiment in the U.K.," it added.

Shoe Zone's revenue fell to 149.1 million pounds from 161.3 million pounds. However, its digital revenue increased slightly to 36.0 million pounds from 35.2 million pounds.

Trading conditions remain challenging on macroeconomic pressure and higher wages, Shoe Zone said. Pretax profit is expected to be around 1.0 million pounds for the year ending Oct. 3, 2026, it said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: ZAWYA-SNG: Bahrain: KFH announces launch of Family Takaful products
data.description:
First published: 13-Jan-2026 12:28:32

Staff Writer

Bahrain - Kuwait Finance House Bahrain, one of the leading providers of Shari’a-compliant banking services in Bahrain, is now an Appointed Representative of Solidarity Bahrain, one of the largest takaful companies in the kingdom, and has announced the launch of Family Takaful products issued by Solidarity Bahrain.

Family Takaful products are a cornerstone of sound personal financial planning, offering customers peace of mind through financial protection and long-term security. With the inclusion of these products, KFH Bahrain enhances its capabilities to provide comprehensive financial solutions to its customers.

Speaking on the launch, Mohamed Zakout, deputy group chief executive officer – retail banking at KFH Bahrain, said: “At KFH, our customers remain at the centre of every decision we make. With the introduction of Family Takaful solutions, we are also providing them with an essential component for comprehensive financial planning – all under one roof. Complementing Family Takaful products with our Shari’a-compliant banking solutions reinforces our role of a trusted lifelong partner.”

On his side, Jawad Mohammed, chief executive officer at Solidarity Bahrain, said: We are delighted to join hands with KFH Bahrain in this strategic partnership, which aligns perfectly with our mission to make Family Takaful solutions more accessible and relevant to customers across the kingdom. We aim to deliver innovative, Shari’a-compliant protection plans that provide real value and long-term financial security. This collaboration reflects our shared commitment to supporting individuals and families at every stage of their financial journey, while strengthening the overall Takaful ecosystem in Bahrain.” 

This collaboration emphasises KFH Bahrain’s ongoing mission to strengthen its ability as a trusted financial partner dedicated to supporting customers at every stage of their financial journey.

data.title: Whitbread rises on expecting lower impact from UK property rate hike
data.description:
** Shares of Premier Inn owner Whitbread jump 4.18% to 2,692p

** WTB expects lower-than-anticipated hit from business rate hikes announced in UK's autumn budget

** Group now expects measures to result in ~35 mln pounds ($47.1 mln) impact in fiscal 2027 vs earlier estimate of 40 mln-50 mln pounds

** Adds, confident in full-year outlook and reaching profitability this year

** In 2025, stock fell ~15%

data.title: Sika falls after FY miss, guidance cut
data.description:
** Shares of Sika AG are down around 7% to their lowest price since November after the Swiss construction chemicals maker reported lower-than-expected full-year results and cut its guidance

** Full-year sales dropped by 4.8% to 11.20 billion Swiss francs ($14.04 billion), missing consensus analyst forecasts of 11.42 billion gathered by Visible Alpha

** "The miss was driven by all regions at the local-currency level, though most materially by APAC and the Americas," RBC analysts say

** Sika said it expects full-year EBITDA margin to be "slightly above 19%" compared with its previous guidance for "19.5-19.8%"

($1 = 0.7975 Swiss francs)
